channel.title: Allen Blog
channel.description: Thoughts on software engineering, product development, and learning.

item.title: Hello, World — Starting My Dev Blog
item.description: The first post on my portfolio blog, covering what I plan to write about and why.

item.title: WSL2 Network Issues: What Caused Them and How I Actually Fixed Them
item.description: WSL2 is excellent until it isn't. The virtualized network stack that makes WSL2 faster than WSL1 for most things is also the source of a class of networking issues that can waste a lot of time to diag

item.title: Migrating React Projects to TypeScript
item.description: Migrating a JavaScript React project to TypeScript is not about changing everything at once. The realistic strategy is to gradually convert files while maintaining stability. This guide covers pattern

item.title: At registration:
item.description: Code review is one of the most valuable — and most time-consuming — parts of software development. A good senior engineer doesn't just spot bugs. They flag architectural issues, question naming choice

item.title: the lean startup by eric ries
item.description: I picked up this book expecting another Silicon Valley hype fest. What I got instead was a surprisingly rigorous methodology that changed how I think about building anything — not just tech companies.

item.title: team agents triple crown strategy
item.description: | Tool | Responsibility | Analogy | |------|---------------|---------| | gstack | Strategy + quality validation | CEO + QA team | | GSD | Project structuring + execution management | PM + Kanban board

item.title: Design Tokens with Tailwind CSS
item.description: Design tokens are variables that represent design decisions — colors, spacing, typography — as code. Combining Tailwind CSS's configuration system with CSS variables allows systematic management of a

item.title: Satellite Communication LEO / MEO / GEO Comparison Analysis
item.description: Satellite communication systems are classified as LEO, MEO, and GEO based on the orbital altitude at which the satellites operate. Each orbit has unique characteristics determined by the laws of physi

item.title: Xilinx RFSoC Platform Overview
item.description: Xilinx RFSoC (Radio Frequency System-on-Chip) is a platform that integrates high-speed ADCs/DACs, an FPGA fabric, and an ARM processor into a single chip. Compared to traditional separate RF front-end

item.title: React Hooks Deep Dive: Real-World Patterns and Pitfalls
item.description: React Hooks manage state and side effects in functional components. Beyond basic usage, this post addresses real-world problems encountered in performance optimization and complex state management.

item.title: 10 Prompt Engineering Tips That Actually Work in Production
item.description: Most prompt engineering advice falls into one of two traps: it's either too vague ('be clear and specific!') or too tied to specific tricks that stop working after a model update. This guide is differ

item.title: Open RAN Architecture Overview
item.description: Open RAN (Open Radio Access Network) is a technology paradigm that separates the hardware and software of mobile base stations and ensures interoperability through open interfaces. It opens up the tra

item.title: mise/tasks/deploy
item.description: One-line verdict: npm scripts are the safe default for JavaScript projects where portability and zero-setup matter, but mise tasks win if you want a proper task runner with dependency graphs, parallel

item.title: Next.js 14 App Router Patterns
item.description: Next.js 14's App Router is a new routing system designed around React Server Components (RSC). It differs conceptually from the pages/ directory approach, and misunderstanding the correct patterns lea

item.title: Military Satellite Communication System Architecture
item.description: Military Satellite Communication (MILSATCOM), unlike commercial satellite communications, has Anti-Jamming (AJ), Low Probability of Intercept/Detection (LPI/LPD), and high availability as core require

item.title: Ctrl+B, D # Detach session (keeps running in background)

item.title: LTE Protocol Stack Architecture Analysis
item.description: The LTE (Long Term Evolution) Radio Access Layer is composed of multiple sublayers. This document summarizes the role and interaction of each layer.

item.title: Understanding JavaScript Closures
item.description: Closures are one of the most frequently misunderstood concepts in JavaScript. They're a staple of interview questions, but more importantly, they're patterns you use every day. This post focuses on ho

item.title: Test it works

item.title: how i made 2000 in 30 days selling ai generated digital products on gumroad
item.description: I'll be straight with you: $2,000 in 30 days sounds like a headline designed to get clicks. But this is a real account of what I did, what it cost, how many hours I put in, and — critically — what alm

item.title: Git Workflow Best Practices
item.description: Git is not just a tool — it's the language of team collaboration. This post covers Git workflow patterns that clearly manage code change history and enable fast collaboration and safe deployment.

item.title: Git Tags — Version Management and Releases
item.description: When working on any serious project, you need a way to mark specific points in your code history — release versions, milestones, stable builds. Git tags do exactly that.

item.title: Git Stash — A Complete Guide to Temporarily Saving Your Work
item.description: Have you ever been deep in the middle of coding a feature, only to get an urgent request to fix a production bug? Or realized you've been working on the wrong branch? Git Stash is the tool that saves

item.title: cursor ai vs github copilot which ai coding assistant is actually worth it
item.description: One-line verdict: Cursor AI wins for developers who want a deeply integrated AI-first coding environment, while GitHub Copilot is the safer, more portable choice for teams already embedded in the GitH

item.title: CSS Grid Mastery Guide
item.description: CSS Grid is a two-dimensional layout system. While Flexbox handles one dimension (row or column), Grid controls rows and columns simultaneously. Its core strength is implementing complex layouts with

item.title: Mastering Team Automation with Claude Code: A Practical Guide to Agents and Remote Control
item.description: This book is a hands-on guide to moving beyond using Claude AI alone and operating multiple AIs simultaneously as a team.

item.title: 5 Next.js App Router Tips I Wish I Knew Earlier
item.description: Practical patterns for layouts, loading states, and data fetching in the App Router.
